content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

The implementation is correct and well-designed. The three-state PidRecordLiveState model (Live/Stale/RunningUnverified) properly addresses all acceptance criteria: (1) macOS/BSD proc_start_marker is honored via the cfg(all(unix, not(target_os="linux"))) path in recorded_process_live_state_with_start_time, (2) legacy pid files without start-time fields safely return RunningUnverified instead of making false liveness claims, (3) PID reuse is detected by comparing live process start times against recorded timestamps with appropriate precision handling (WholeSeconds for non-Linux, Precise for Linux). All callers correctly handle the three states — safety-critical operations (stop, kill, reclaim) refuse to act on RunningUnverified, while liveness guards (resume blocking, orchestrator alive check, interrupted handoff wait) conservatively treat RunningUnverified as potentially alive. The CliWriterCleanupHandoff.recorded_at addition is backward-compatible via serde(default). All tests pass (841 passed, 0 failed), clippy is clean, fmt passes, and nix build succeeds.

## Amendments

None.
